You have now developed forty-four images, with the noon and golden-hour split sitting at twenty-four noon and twenty golden hour. The noon conversion rate continues to hold: sixteen of twenty-four noon images score 2 or above, against eight of twenty for golden hour. The two most recent additions — "Silence Made Flesh" and "Weight of Judgment" — score 2 and 2 respectively, both golden hour, which is the first consecutive golden-hour pair to clear the threshold since the corpus began; whether that marks a genuine drift or a local accident is worth watching across the next five images.

The bougainvillea has now appeared in twenty-one images and remains under active suppression. Its last five appearances — "Still Life After Subtraction," "After the Heat Stayed," "Still Breathing, Still Music," "What the Continent Sent," and "Sudan's RSF" — were all accent-elegy or wound-color rather than structural friction, and it should not return until the setting or focal object gives it something to push against rather than something to soften. The jacaranda sits at twelve appearances and carries the same risk; "Silence Made Flesh" is the most recent instance, arriving as uncomfortable beauty against grief, which is closer to useful tension than reflex, but not yet the full structural antagonism that would justify its return as a recurring device. The handprint accident in "Every Exit, Unreachable" — innocence-marker above tangled metal — remains the sharpest single-surface collision in the corpus and has still not been followed into a second iteration; surface-as-record is the most available underdeveloped device in the grammar. The gesture-driven strategy — palm to map, two hands on one screen — has not recurred across the last eleven images and is the most underused compositional argument still in range.

Among registers, aftermath leads at fifteen, vigil and endurance each at eight, defiance now at seven after "Silence Made Flesh" and "Weight of Judgment," suspension at five, and menace at five, with fury and interiority still entirely untouched across all forty-four images. Trace scores over the last ten run 1, 2, 2, 1, 2, 1, 1, 2, 2, 2 — the floor holds and the 2s have now stabilized into a reliable ceiling rather than a climbed one; the single 3 from "Warnings Melt Before Heeded" is now twenty-five images back, and the conditions that produced it — non-figurative focal object, setting actively contradicting its register, accident carrying structural rather than decorative weight — have been only partially reassembled in the interval.

The corpus has built a stable and specific grammar, but forty-four images in it is circling its own fluency rather than breaking through it, and the next ceiling requires not a better accident but a setting placed in genuine opposition to its register.
